region_of_origin: Singapore (served); oat milk brand of Dutch/global origin, mango flavour
food_subgroup: Flavoured oat milk beverage; mango-flavoured plant-based drink

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for mango-flavoured oat milk beverage. FOODON_03301737 (oat milk food product) used as closest parent. The promotional sticker component has no nutritional relevance and is excluded from ontology mapping.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC (oat-based beverages) is the closest defensible parent. No specific code exists for mango-flavoured oat milk drinks in FoodEx2 as of 2026.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code identified for mango-flavoured oat milk beverage. SNOMED CT does not have a specific concept for this product category.

serving.natural_language_portion: One serving is approximately one cup (350 g / 350 ml) of the Oat Mango Boom beverage as served at HEYTEA, Marina Bay Sands, Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Oat milk, plain (unsweetened) — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy; adapted with mango-flavoured sweetened oat milk adjustments from USDA Branded Food entries for flavoured oat milk beverages
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA or HPB entry exists for HEYTEA Oat Mango Boom. Nutrient values are estimated by combining USDA FoodData Central data for oat milk (NDB 14640 / FDC ID 1097542) with typical mango-flavoured sweetened oat milk branded product profiles from USDA Branded Food entries (e.g., FDC ID 2097234 — Oatly Oat Drink, and comparable mango oat milk products). Sugar content is elevated relative to plain oat milk to reflect mango flavouring and added sugars typical of HEYTEA-style beverages. Values represent a best-estimate per 100 g of the beverage component only; the sticker has no nutritional value.

gi_source.title: Oat milk beverage GI analogous estimate — based on oat-based drinks GI data; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; peer-reviewed journal (Diabetes Care)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for HEYTEA Oat Mango Boom. GI estimated at 60 (medium) based on oat milk beverage GI values reported in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care), which report oat-based beverages in the GI range 55–69. Added mango flavouring and sugar content support a medium GI classification. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (60 × 43.75) / 100 = 26.25, rounded to 26.3. Carbohydrate per serving = 12.5 g/100g × 350 g = 43.75 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Moderate sodium content (~45 mg/100 g; ~158 mg per 350 ml serving)
hypertension.guidance: The sodium content of one serving (~158 mg) is modest and unlikely to significantly impact blood pressure in hypertensive individuals when consumed occasionally, though regular consumption of sweetened beverages should be monitored as part of overall dietary sodium and caloric intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar content (~9 g/100 g; ~31.5 g added and natural sugars per 350 ml serving) and medium glycemic index (~60)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should exercise caution with this beverage due to its substantial sugar load (~31.5 g per serving) and medium GI of approximately 60, which can cause a significant postprandial blood glucose rise; portion control or avoidance is recommended.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (0.1 g/100 g), dairy-free, plant-based oat base; however, high sugar content is a secondary concern
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The low saturated fat and dairy-free profile of this oat-based beverage is broadly favourable for cardiovascular health, but the high added sugar content (~31.5 g per serving) warrants moderation as excess sugar intake is associated with dyslipidaemia and increased cardiovascular risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The OATSIDE Sticker Set is a limited promotional offering available at HEYTEA (Marina Bay Sands, Singapore) via field survey data, consisting of one Oat Mango Boom beverage and one collectible sticker. The Oat Mango Boom is a chilled, commercially prepared mango-flavoured oat milk drink — a plant-based beverage made from oat milk blended with mango puree or mango flavouring and sweetened with added sugars. The drink is dairy-free, vegan, and contains oats (a gluten-containing grain). It has a creamy yet light texture with a sweet tropical mango flavour balanced by the mild nuttiness of oat milk. Nutritionally, per 100 g the beverage provides approximately 62 kcal, 0.8 g protein, 1.2 g fat (predominantly unsaturated), and 12.5 g carbohydrate of which approximately 9 g is sugar. A standard 350 ml serving delivers roughly 217 kcal and 31.5 g of sugar, making it a moderate-to-high calorie beverage with a significant sugar load. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 60 (medium GI), yielding a glycemic load of approximately 26 per serving — clinically significant for individuals managing blood glucose. The oat base provides beta-glucan dietary fibre (approximately 0.5 g/100 g), which may offer modest cholesterol-lowering benefits, but the high sugar content offsets many of the health benefits associated with plain oat milk. Clinically, this beverage is of concern for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its sugar content and medium GI. It is broadly acceptable for hypertensive individuals given its modest sodium content. The promotional sticker component has no nutritional value and is excluded from all nutrient calculations. Priced at SGD 11.50, this is a premium beverage set positioned in the dessert and bubble tea category in Singapore's food delivery market.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Oat Mango Boom?
Is HEYTEA Oat Mango Boom dairy-free?
What is the sugar content of Oat Mango Boom?
Can diabetics drink Oat Mango Boom?
What is the GI of oat mango drink?

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional data exists for HEYTEA Oat Mango Boom in any approved primary source (HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, ICMR-NIN, or FAO/INFOODS SEA). Values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central oat milk entries and comparable branded flavoured oat milk products. The GI is an analogous estimate based on oat-based beverage data from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008. Confidence is therefore low. The promotional sticker component has no nutritional relevance.
notes: 1. The OATSIDE Sticker Set is a promotional bundle: one Oat Mango Boom beverage plus one collectible sticker. All nutrient values apply to the beverage component only; the sticker has no nutritional value and is excluded from all calculations. 2. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero by formulation as oat milk-based plant beverages do not contain industrially produced trans fats; naturally zero for this product category. 3. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 — naturally zero as this is a plant-based dairy-free beverage containing no animal-derived ingredients. 4. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reliably reported in the USDA oat milk reference entries used for estimation. 5. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this product category. 6. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this product category. 7. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this product category. 8. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this product category. 9. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this product category. 10. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this product category. 11. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this product category. 12. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this product category. 13. phosphorus_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this product category. 14. magnesium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this product category. 15. zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this product category. 16. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this product category. 17. glycemic_index set to 60 (medium) as an analogous estimate based on oat-based beverage GI data from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care); no direct GI measurement exists for this specific product. 18. This food is classified under Singapore cuisine context for multilingual naming purposes as it is served in Singapore, though the OATSIDE brand has Dutch/global origins. 19. The food_name has been expanded from the promotional set name to a more canonical descriptive form for database clarity; the original field survey name 'OATSIDE Sticker Set' is preserved in aliases.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Expanded food_name from promotional set name to canonical descriptive English form; added full multilingual local_names for Singapore cuisine context (Chinese simplified/traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi); populated all nutrient fields with USDA-based estimates; added GI analogous estimate from approved sources; documented all -1 sentinel values in notes; verified aliases_by_language consistency with flat aliases; added zero-macro justifications for trans_fat_g and cholesterol_mg; set ontology match_type values to approved set only.

Iteration 2: Corrected a mixed-script Hindi alias in `aliases_by_language` from 'ओट मैंगो बூम' (containing a Tamil character) to 'ओट मैंगो बूम' (pure Devanagari), ensuring exact consistency with the flat `aliases` array and maintaining script purity. This resolves the Q8 failure.
